content.text: I'll start by inspecting the cluster state.

input.command: echo "pw:"; kubectl get secret elastic-password -n elasticsearch -o jsonpath='{.data.password}'|base64 -d;echo; echo "next:"; kubectl get secret elastic-password-next -n elasticsearch -o jsonpath='{.data.password}'|base64 -d;echo; echo "===STS es spec==="; kubectl get sts es -n elasticsearch -o yaml 2>&1 | grep -iE "keystore|secure|secret|image:|volumeMount|mountPath|name:" | head -50

input.command:
PW=$(kubectl get secret elastic-password -n elasticsearch -o jsonpath='{.data.password}'|base64 -d)
kubectl exec -n elasticsearch es-0 -c elasticsearch -- bash -c "curl -s -k -u elastic:$PW https://localhost:9200/_cat/nodes?v" 2>&1
echo "===es-data sts==="
kubectl get sts es-data -n elasticsearch -o jsonpath='{.spec.template.spec.containers[0].env}' 2>&1 | tr ',' '\n' | grep -iE "cluster.name|node|s3|secure"

content.text: It's one 5-node cluster. Let me verify MinIO bucket and create the secure-settings secret.

input.command:
# Confirm minio creds and bucket via minio-init logs
kubectl logs -n elasticsearch minio-init-2grqf 2>&1 | tail -15
echo "===root creds==="
kubectl get secret minio-root -n elasticsearch -o jsonpath='{.data.rootUser}'|base64 -d; echo " / "; kubectl get secret minio-root -n elasticsearch -o jsonpath='{.data.rootPassword}'|base64 -d; echo

content.text: No MinIO service exists and the bucket was never created — that's the root cause. Let me create the service, the bucket, and the secret.

input.command:
cat <<'EOF' | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: minio
  namespace: elasticsearch
spec:
  selector:
    app: minio
  ports:
  - name: api
    port: 9000
    targetPort: 9000
EOF
echo "===create bucket dir==="
kubectl exec -n elasticsearch minio-6866497fcb-vr47x -- mkdir -p /data/es-backups 2>&1
kubectl exec -n elasticsearch minio-6866497fcb-vr47x -- ls /data 2>&1
echo "===create secret==="
kubectl create secret generic es-secure-settings -n elasticsearch \
  --from-literal=s3.client.default.access_key=minioadmin \
  --from-literal=s3.client.default.secret_key=minioadmin 2>&1

===create bucket dir===
es-backups
===create secret===
secret/es-secure-settings created

content.text: Now configure the keystore on all 5 ES nodes from the secret, then reload.

input.command:
AK=$(kubectl get secret es-secure-settings -n elasticsearch -o jsonpath='{.data.s3\.client\.default\.access_key}'|base64 -d)
SK=$(kubectl get secret es-secure-settings -n elasticsearch -o jsonpath='{.data.s3\.client\.default\.secret_key}'|base64 -d)
for p in es-0 es-1 es-2 es-data-0 es-data-1; do
  echo "=== $p ==="
  kubectl exec -n elasticsearch $p -c elasticsearch -- bash -c "
    cd /usr/share/elasticsearch
    printf '%s' '$AK' | bin/elasticsearch-keystore add --stdin --force s3.client.default.access_key 2>&1
    printf '%s' '$SK' | bin/elasticsearch-keystore add --stdin --force s3.client.default.secret_key 2>&1
    bin/elasticsearch-keystore list | grep s3" 2>&1
done

input.command:
PW=$(kubectl get secret elastic-password -n elasticsearch -o jsonpath='{.data.password}'|base64 -d)
echo "===reload secure settings==="
kubectl exec -n elasticsearch es-0 -c elasticsearch -- bash -c "curl -s -k -u elastic:$PW -X POST https://localhost:9200/_nodes/reload_secure_settings" 2>&1; echo
echo "===check minio DNS reachability from es-0==="
kubectl exec -n elasticsearch es-0 -c elasticsearch -- bash -c "curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' http://minio.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local:9000/minio/health/live" 2>&1; echo

content.text: All 5 nodes reloaded and MinIO is reachable. Now register the repository and create a snapshot.

input.command:
PW=$(kubectl get secret elastic-password -n elasticsearch -o jsonpath='{.data.password}'|base64 -d)
echo "===register minio-repo==="
kubectl exec -n elasticsearch es-0 -c elasticsearch -- bash -c "curl -s -k -u elastic:$PW -X PUT https://localhost:9200/_snapshot/minio-repo -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{
  \"type\":\"s3\",
  \"settings\":{
    \"bucket\":\"es-backups\",
    \"endpoint\":\"minio.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local:9000\",
    \"protocol\":\"http\",
    \"path_style_access\":true,
    \"client\":\"default\"
  }
}'" 2>&1; echo
echo "===verify repo==="
kubectl exec -n elasticsearch es-0 -c elasticsearch -- bash -c "curl -s -k -u elastic:$PW -X POST https://localhost:9200/_snapshot/minio-repo/_verify" 2>&1; echo
